title: maytenus revoluta subsp. macrophylla
intro: Maytenus revoluta subsp. macrophylla, also known as the 'Andean mayten', is a plant species that belongs to the Celastraceae family, native to the Andes mountain range in South America. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Maytenus revoluta subsp. macrophylla include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, as well as its use as an anti-diarrheal agent. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve the roots and stems, which are dried and then used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures.
